rulings.Case Title: INFORMATION DELIVERY SERVICES INC VS. ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 3, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT ATM EQUITY PARTNERS, LLC, CRAIG WICHNER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Mr. Wichner's motion for summary judgment on behalf is denied and defendants' alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues one, two and four and moot as to issue three. Mr. Wichner is not entitled to summary judgment since his motion is only based on the alter ego doctrine yet he is sued also for his individual participation in the alleged wrongful acts. As to issue one, Mr. Wichner satisfied his initial burden that adherence to the separateness of Mr. Wichner and ATM would not produce an inequitable result and Plaintiff failed to raise a triable issue. As to issues two and four, Defendants satisfied their initial burdens and Plaintiff failed to raise a triable issues. Defendants' Objections to Evidence five, six, seven and ten are sustained. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH KARNAZES VS. SAINT PAUL TRAVELERS INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 3, 2012, Line 4. DEFENDANT ANDERLINI FINKELSTEIN EMERICK AND SMOOT, DAVID FINKELSTEIN DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Moving party fails to demonstrate that the two actions involve the same parties and the same causes of action. Moving party's remedy, if any, is file a motion per Code of Civil Procedure 597 and the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: XI LUAN et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (CCSF) et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Fri, 3, 2012 Line 1 - PLAINTIFFS' Motion for order establishing admissions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: RONALD J. VAN POOL VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 3, 2012, Line 24. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment. Granted. Defendant presented a legitimate reason (i.e. that plaintiff has not passed the Physical Agility Test ("PAT") for not promoting the plaintiff. Plaintiff has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue that that this reason was pretextual. For the purposes of this motion, Plaintiff stipulates that he has not needed an accommodation since August 2007. The undisputed evidence is that any harassment of plaintiff was neither severe nor pervasive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHABAD OF S. FRANCISCO, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. ABRAHAM AMAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And For Production Of Documetns, Set One By Plntf And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Fri. Feb. 3, 2012 Line 6 - PLAINTIFF CHABAD OF S. FRANCISCO, INC., A CALIFORNIA Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And For Production Of Documetns, Set One By Plntf And Sanctions, Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Court finds Defendants' responses do not comply with the requirements of CCP §2131.210-240, et. seq. Defendants to provide verified supplemental responses within 15 days stating that "after a diligent search, all responsive documents have been produced" in response to the outstanding requests. Both party's requests for sanctions are denied. Moving party to submit proposed order to Dept 301 no later than 9:00 a.m. on Friday, February 3, 2012 for signature by the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE MEULEMANS VS. LEA G SHAPIRO et al (TRANSFERRED TO SMALL CLAIMS PER ORDER FEB-03-12)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 3, 2012, Line 17. PLAINTIFF CATHERINE MEULEMANS Notice Of Motion For Order Reclassifying Case As Small Claims Matter. Granted. No opposition filed. The case will be transferred to the small claims division. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)
